mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in real retail sales (RSAFS) signals weakening consumer demand, which depresses corporate revenues and earnings, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If RSAFS month-over-month declines are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of instances over a 2-year rolling window, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate (UNRATE) exceeds 4.0%, the labor market is in a distressed state that reduces aggregate demand and earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: Over any 3-year period, instances where UNRATE > 4.0% yield median 20-day forward returns greater than zero, or rank IC below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous monthly decline in both industrial production (INDPRO) and retail sales (RSAFS) signals a synchronized demand-supply contraction that deepens recession expectations, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: Both INDPRO and RSAFS post negative month-over-month changes
failure_condition: No statistically significant difference in 60-day forward returns between joint-decline periods and other periods (t-test p >= 0.05) over rolling 5-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is at or below 0.5% (zero lower bound), the economy faces liquidity trap conditions where additional monetary easing is ineffective; deflation expectations rise, depressing nominal earnings and leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 days.
regime_hint: zero lower bound with deflation risk
failure_condition: If during periods with FEDFUNDS <= 0.5%, the average 20-day forward return is positive and statistically significant (t-test p < 0.05), the hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: A low level of industrial production (INDPRO), defined as below 100 index points, is a contrarian signal: mean reversion forces and anticipated policy stimulus lift equities over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: If INDPRO below 100 is followed by a negative average 5-day return or if the rank IC over a 2-year window is non-positive.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline of more than 5% in housing starts (HOUST) signals a sharp contraction in residential investment, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days due to weakening real estate and consumer confidence.
failure_condition: If in periods with HOUST m/m < -5% the 20-day forward return is positive more than 50% of the time, the hypothesis is wrong.

mechanism_narrative: Consecutive months of rising unemployment rate (UNRATE) and declining payroll employment (PAYEMS) indicate a severe labor market deterioration that reduces consumer spending and corporate earnings, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE increases and PAYEMS decreases over two consecutive months
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative returns significantly (mean return difference < 0 in 60-day forward returns between triggered and non-triggered periods) over any 5-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When the CPI (CPIAUCSL) declines month-over-month (disinflation) and the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) is below 0.5%, the economy faces deflation expectations that erode nominal earnings power, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Over any 3-year rolling period, the average 20-day forward return during the triggered regime is non-negative, or the Sharpe ratio of the signal is not less than zero.
